data.content:
HANDOFF CAPSULE (foolery-6881) — take-loop onTurnEnded wiring

Commit: 1e32e0d4 on origin/main.

Why this knot existed:
The user asserted this bug was "definitely 100% fixed" in prior
conversations, yet the fix-for-all-takes was still missing on main.
foolery-a401 removed the payload-shape gate and made onTurnEnded
payload-agnostic, but only the INITIAL child in
src/lib/terminal-manager-initial-io.ts:62 actually wired an
onTurnEnded handler. The take-loop runtime in
src/lib/terminal-manager-take-child.ts was still calling
createSessionRuntime without one, so iterations 2, 3, 4... of
the take-loop had zero in-session follow-up capability. That is
the exact "this only works on the first take" variant of the bug
that has now been fake-fixed twice. That is why the tag
"fake-fixed-already" is on this knot — intentionally.

Where the take-loop runtime was wired (file:line):
- src/lib/terminal-manager-take-child.ts:214-220
  createTakeRuntimeBundle now passes onTurnEnded:
  () => invokeTakeLoopTurnEnded(ctx, httpRefs) into
  createSessionRuntime. The invoker (lines 237-249) reads the
  deferred child + runtime refs that were already maintained for
  the HTTP transport and returns Promise<boolean>.

Handler file and logic:
- src/lib/terminal-manager-take-follow-up.ts
  handleTakeLoopTurnEnded(ctx, runtime, child):
    1. Fetches current beat state via getBackend().get.
    2. If fetch fails → return false (let grace-period close run).
    3. resolveWorkflowForBeat with the live state, then
       isQueueOrTerminal(state, workflow):
       - If true → agent advanced the knot → return false.
       - If false → active state → build a followup prompt with
         buildTakeLoopFollowUpPrompt (embedding beat id + state),
         call runtime.sendUserTurn(child, prompt,
         "take_loop_follow_up"), log
         recordTakeLoopLifecycle("take_loop_follow_up_sent", ...)
         and push a styled stdout line with the reason. Return
         true on send success, false on send failure (logged).

Signature widening (Option C per spec):
- src/lib/agent-session-runtime-types.ts — onTurnEnded is now
  () => boolean | Promise<boolean>.
- src/lib/agent-session-runtime.ts — handleTurnEnded awaits the
  Promise branch; grace-period doScheduleInputClose runs only
  after the async handler resolves to false (or throws). The
  sync branch is preserved, so the initial-io caller (still
  returning plain boolean) keeps foolery-a401 behavior byte-for-
  byte.

Lifecycle event extension:
- src/lib/terminal-manager-take-lifecycle.ts — added
  "take_loop_follow_up_sent" to TakeLoopLifecycleEvent plus a
  takeLoopFollowUpSentAt field + applyEvent case.

Tests (src/lib/__tests__/terminal-manager-take-loop-follow-up.test.ts):
- Line 116: Test A — active beat state triggers sendUserTurn
  with a prompt containing "still in state" and the beat id;
  source is "take_loop_follow_up".
- Line 142: Test B — terminal state ("shipped") returns false
  and does NOT call sendUserTurn, letting the runtime's
  grace-period close run.
- Line 163: Test C — sendUserTurn returning false causes the
  handler to log a warning and return false so the runtime
  falls back to input-close.
- Line 185: extra — backend fetch failure returns false safely.
- Line 215: buildTakeLoopFollowUpPrompt embeds id + state.
- Line 234: Test D (CANARY) — calls createTakeRuntimeBundle
  directly and asserts the returned runtime.config.onTurnEnded
  is a function. If a future refactor drops this wiring, this
  test fails loudly. To make this assertion possible,
  createTakeRuntimeBundle is now exported from
  terminal-manager-take-child.ts.

Quality gates run in worktree
/Users/cartine/foolery/.worktrees/foolery-6881:
- bun run lint                — clean
- bunx tsc --noEmit           — clean
- bun run test                — 2733 pass, 11 skip, 42 todo
- bun run build               — success

DO-NOT-FAKE-FIX WARNING:
If you are tempted to skip wiring `onTurnEnded` in a new
take-loop runtime factory or to return `false` unconditionally
in handleTakeLoopTurnEnded, STOP. That is the exact fake-fix
this knot eradicates. Test D is the canary — do not patch it,
audit the wiring instead.

Knot chain (context):
- foolery-557d — investigation
- foolery-2782 — watchdog firing log
- foolery-e750 — enriched child_close log
- foolery-062f — terminateProcessGroup entry log
- foolery-f8ae — E2E watchdog observability chain test
- foolery-a401 — onResult->onTurnEnded rename + payload-agnostic
                 + per-transport trigger
- foolery-6881 — THIS KNOT: wire the take-loop consumer
